zh.lifeSummary: 當Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla於1275年4月25日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯時，他的父親Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"19歲，母親Blanche de France22歲。他於1308年與Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1322年6月1日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安塞戈維亞享年47歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla于1275年4月25日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯时，他的父亲Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"19岁，母亲Blanche de France22岁。他于1308年与Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1322年6月1日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安塞戈維亞享年47岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Cerda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Francisco, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Ramon, Guadalupe, Ricardo, Miguel, Pedro, Carlos.

Spanish and Portuguese: from cerda ‘bristle, hair’ (from Late Latin cirra), apparently a nickname for someone with a prominent tuft of hair. One of the sons of King Alfonso X (1221–84) was known as Fernando de la Cerda.
Catalan (Cerdà): habitational name, cerdà, for someone from La Cerdanya, a Catalan district in the Pyrenees which is situated partly in Spain and partly in France. Compare Serda .

variants.name: Serda
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Agustin, Angel, Humberto, Juan, Lupe, Manuel, Reynaldo, Santos, Adela, Ana, Angelina, Bacilio.
Catalan:
(Serdà): variant of Cerdà (see Cerda ).

nameMeanings.name: Castilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Carlos, Raul, Sergio, Jesus, Jorge, Pablo, Ruben, Blanca, Elido, Francisco, Guillermo.
Spanish: habitational name from Castile (Spanish Castilla), a region in central Spain. The name derives from the many castles in the region. This surname is also found in southern France.
History: As an independent kingdom between the 10th and 15th centuries, Castile formed the largest power in the Iberian peninsula.

variants.name: Castiglia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonino, Benedetto, Carmine, Francesco, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Ignazio.
Italian: habitational name from Castile (Italian Castiglia) in central Spain (see Castilla ).

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Hernán
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

variants.name: Hernando
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla was born on 25 April 1275, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla", was 19 and his mother, Blanche de France, was 22. He married Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, in 1308. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 1 June 1322, in Segovia, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 47, and was buried in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla est né le 25 avril 1275, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla", avait 19 ans et sa mère, Blanche de France, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, en 1308. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 1 juin 1322, à Segovia, Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla am 25. April 1275 in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" 19 und seine Mutter Blanche de France 22. Er hat 1308 Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 1. Juni 1322 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Segovia, Castilla y León, Spanien und wurde in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla è nato il il 25 aprile 1275 nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna, il suo padre, Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla", aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Blanche de France, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, nel 1308. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1322, nel Segovia, Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Fernando II de la Cerda y Castillaが1275年4月25日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスで生まれた時，父Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"は19歳，母Blanche de Franceは22歳であった。1308年にJuana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1322年6月1日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンセゴヴィアで47歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla(이)가 1275년 4월 25일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Blanche de France(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1308년 Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1322년 6월 1일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 세고비아에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에 묻혔다.

pt.25 de abril de 1275–1 Jun 1322: 25 de abril de 1275–1 de junho de 1322
pt.Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Ma: Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha
pt.Burgos, Burgos, Castilla-Leon, Spain: Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha
pt.Lerma, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain: Lerma, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla nasceu em 25 de abril de 1275, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla", tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Blanche de France, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, em 1308. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de junho de 1322, em Segovia, Castilla y León, Espanha, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla родился 25 апреля 1275 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Blanche de France, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, в 1308 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 1 июня 1322 г. в местности сеговя, кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Fernando II de la Cerda y Castilla nació el 25 de abril de 1275, en Burgos, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla", tenía 19 años y su mamá, Blanche de France, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, en 1308. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1322, en Segovia, Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Burgos, Castilla y León, España.
